feed.title: avianna.ai
feed.subtitle: Notes on math, AI, and software.

entry.title: Better Models, Different Risks
entry.summary: Better models shrink some enterprise risks and grow others. Capability is not authority: how reach, velocity, and blast radius change the shape of agent risk.

entry.title: The worker and the action
entry.summary: An agent gets neither the onboarding a new hire gets nor the sign-offs a transaction gets. How to build both, and why one without the other fails.

entry.title: The problem
entry.summary: Onboarding digital labor is the hardest challenge of the agent era. A prompt configures a model. It does not onboard a worker.

entry.title: The framework
entry.summary: What a properly onboarded agent inherits: a role, context, permissions, policy, evaluation, and a path to more trust.

entry.title: In practice
entry.summary: An agent traced through Lattice end to end, graduating from observing to acting on its own without losing accountability.

entry.title: Why agents need a contract
entry.summary: Part 1 of three on Concord. Durable execution tells you what ran. It doesn't tell you what it meant, and that gap matters more now that agents propose work. With an interactive scattered-evidence simulation.

entry.title: The Visible Pursuit
entry.summary: Why every action you take to get what you want tells someone else what you want. Goal recognition, with two interactive simulations.

entry.title: Contract, not runtime
entry.summary: Part 2 of three. Concord is not DBOS, not LangGraph, not OPA. Three small interactive demos draw the layer boundary: a clickable layer stack, an audit-tier picker, and an SVG capability graph.

entry.title: A day in the life
entry.summary: Part 3 of three. A hotel booking traced through Concord end-to-end. Animated nine-step playback with three modes: full contract, minimal subset, no Concord at all.
